24 SECTION 612. IC 25-4-0.5 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA CODE
25 AS A NEW CHAPTER TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
26 JANUARY 1, 2027]:
27 Chapter 0.5. Definitions
28 Sec. 1. The definitions in this chapter apply throughout this
29 article.
30 Sec. 2. "Approved engineering curriculum" means an
31 engineering curriculum of four (4) years or more that has been
32 approved by the board. In approving the engineering curriculum,
33 the board may take into consideration the standards of
34 accreditation adopted by the Engineering Accreditation
35 Commission of the Accreditation Board for Engineering and
36 Technology.
37 Sec. 3. "Board" refers to the Indiana board of registration for
38 design professionals established by IC 25-4-1-2.
39 Sec. 4. "Engineering intern" means an individual who:
40 (1) is a graduate from an approved engineering curriculum of
41 four (4) years or more or who has acquired, through
42 engineering education and experience in engineering work,
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

350
1 knowledge and skill approximating that obtained by
2 graduation in an approved engineering curriculum of four (4)
3 years or more;
4 (2) has successfully passed an examination as prescribed in
5 IC 25-4-3-5; and
6 (3) has been issued by the board an appropriate certificate of
7 enrollment as an engineering intern.
8 Sec. 5. "Landscape architecture" means the practice of
9 professional services such as consultation, investigation,
10 reconnaissance, research, planning, design, or responsible
11 supervision to develop land areas for the dominant purpose of
12 preserving, enhancing, or determining:
13 (1) proper land uses;
14 (2) natural land features;
15 (3) ground cover and planting;
16 (4) naturalistic and aesthetic values;
17 (5) the settings and approaches to structures or other
18 improvements;
19 (6) the natural environment of a facility, an individual
20 building, or other structure;
21 (7) site specific natural surface and subsoil drainage systems;
22 (8) landscape grading, swales, curbs, and walkways; and
23 (9) any inherent problems of the land relating to erosion,
24 overuse, blight, or other hazards.
25 The term includes the location and arrangement of the proposed
26 tangible objects and features that are incidental and necessary to
27 accomplish the purposes of landscape architecture.
28 Sec. 6. "Licensing agency" means the Indiana professional
29 licensing agency established by IC 25-1-5-3.
30 Sec. 7. "Practice of engineering" means any service or creative
31 work that the adequate performance of requires engineering
32 education, training, and experience in the application of special
33 knowledge of the mathematical, physical, and engineering sciences
34 to services or creative work that includes the following:
35 (1) Consultation.
36 (2) Investigation.
37 (3) Evaluation.
38 (4) Planning, including planning the use of land and water.
39 (5) The design of or the supervision of the design of
40 engineering works and systems.
41 (6) Engineering surveys and studies or the supervision of
42 engineering surveys and studies, including all surveying
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

351
1 activities required to support the sound conception, planning,
2 design, construction, maintenance, and operation of
3 engineered projects, but not including the surveying of real
4 property for the establishment of land boundaries,
5 subdivisions, rights-of-way, easements, and the dependent or
6 independent surveys or resurveys of the public land survey
7 system.
8 (7) Evaluation of construction for the purpose of assuring
9 compliance with specifications, plans, and designs, in
10 connection with any public or private utilities, structures,
11 buildings, machines, equipment, processes, work systems, or
12 projects.
13 The term "practice of engineering" does not include the work
14 ordinarily performed by persons who operate or maintain
15 machinery or equipment.
16 Sec. 8. "Practice or offer to practice engineering" means the act
17 of an individual or a business who by verbal claim, sign,
18 advertisement, letterhead, card, telephone listing, or in any other
19 way represents the individual or the business to be a professional
20 engineer or who performs, or offers to perform, any acts or work
21 involving the practice of engineering.
22 Sec. 9. "Practitioner" means an individual registered as a
23 landscape architect under this article.
24 Sec. 10. "Professional engineer" means an individual who, by
25 reason of that individual's special knowledge of the mathematical
26 and physical sciences and the principles and methods of
27 engineering analysis and design which are acquired by education
28 and practical experience, is qualified to engage in the practice of
29 engineering as attested by that individual's registration as a
30 professional engineer.

11 SECTION 622. IC 25-4-3 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA CODE
12 AS A NEW CHAPTER TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
13 JANUARY 1, 2027]:
14 Chapter 3. Regulation of Engineers
15 Sec. 1. The board shall enforce and administer the provisions of
16 this chapter, and adopt rules, not inconsistent with the Constitution
17 and laws of this state, as may be reasonably necessary for the
18 proper performance of its duties and the regulations of the
19 proceedings before it. The board shall adopt rules establishing
20 standards for the competent practice of engineering and for the
21 administration of the registered professional engineers and
22 registered engineering interns investigative fund established by
23 section 20 of this chapter. Any rulemaking by the board shall be in
24 accordance with IC 4-22-2.
25 Sec. 2. (a) Except as provided in IC 25-31-1-35 and subsection
26 (b), the licensing agency shall receive and account for all money
27 collected under the provisions of this chapter and shall deposit the
28 money with the treasurer of state to be deposited by the treasurer
29 of state in the general fund of the state.
30 (b) In addition to the registration fee established under section
31 13(c) of this chapter, the board shall establish a fee of not more
32 than twenty dollars ($20) for registered professional engineers and
33 registered engineering interns to be paid at the time of:
34 (1) issuance of a certificate of registration; and
35 (2) renewal of a certificate of registration;
36 under this chapter to provide funds for administering and
37 enforcing this chapter, including investigating and taking action
38 against persons violating this chapter. All funds collected under
39 this subsection shall be deposited into the registered professional
40 engineers and registered engineering interns investigative fund
41 established by section 20 of this chapter.
42 Sec. 3. (a) The following under either subdivision (1) or (2) shall
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

358
1 be considered as minimum evidence that the applicant is qualified
2 for registration as a professional engineer:
3 (1) All of the following:
4 (A) Graduation in an approved engineering curriculum of
5 four (4) years or more.
6 (B) A specific record of four (4) years or more of
7 progressive experience on engineering projects of sufficient
8 quality acquired subsequent to graduation, which
9 experience indicates that the applicant is qualified to be
10 placed in responsible charge of engineering work requiring
11 the exercise of judgment in the application of engineering
12 sciences to the sound solution of engineering problems.
13 (C) The successful passing of an examination as provided
14 for in section 5 of this chapter.
15 (2) All of the following:
16 (A) A specific record of eight (8) years or more of
17 engineering education and experience in engineering work,
18 which indicates that the applicant has acquired knowledge
19 and skill and practical experience in engineering work
20 approximating that required for registration as a
21 professional engineer under subdivision (1).
22 (B) The successful passing of an examination as provided
23 for in section 5 of this chapter.
24 (b) The following under either subdivision (1) or (2) shall be
25 considered as minimum evidence that the applicant is qualified for
26 certification as an engineering intern:
27 (1) All of the following:
28 (A) Graduation in an approved engineering curriculum of
29 four (4) years or more.
30 (B) The successful passing of an engineering intern
31 examination as provided in section 5 of this chapter.
32 (2) All of the following:
33 (A) A specific record of four (4) years or more of
34 engineering education and experience in engineering work
35 indicating that the applicant has acquired knowledge and
36 skill approximating that acquired through graduation in an
37 approved engineering curriculum of four (4) years or more.
38 (B) The successful passing of an engineering intern
39 examination as provided in section 5 of this chapter.
40 (c) The board may waive the examination under section 5 of this
41 chapter to any applicant who has been granted a certificate of
42 registration under section 13 of this chapter.
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

359
1 (d) An individual is not eligible for registration as a professional
2 engineer or certification as an engineering intern if the individual
3 has been convicted of:
4 (1) an act which would constitute grounds for disciplinary
5 sanction under IC 25-1-11; or
6 (2) a felony that has a direct bearing on the individual's ability
7 to practice competently.
8 (e) In considering the qualifications of applicants, responsible
9 charge of engineering teaching shall be construed as responsible
10 charge of engineering work. An applicant who holds a degree of
11 master of science of engineering or the equivalent thereof from a
12 curriculum in engineering approved by the board may be given a
13 maximum credit of one (1) year of experience in addition to the
14 credit of four (4) years of education. An applicant who holds a
15 degree of doctor of philosophy or the equivalent thereof from a
16 curriculum in engineering approved by the board may be given a
17 maximum of credit of two (2) years of experience in addition to a
18 credit of four (4) years of education. Graduation in a course other
19 than engineering from a college or university acceptable to the
20 board may be considered as equivalent to two (2) years engineering
21 experience and education. The mere execution, as a contractor of
22 work designed by a professional engineer, or the supervision of the
23 construction of such work as a foreman or superintendent shall not
24 be deemed to be engineering experience.
25 (f) Any person having the necessary qualifications to be
26 registered under this chapter is eligible for registration although
27 the individual may not be engaged in the practice of engineering at
28 the time the application is made.
29 Sec. 4. (a) An individual applying for registration as a
30 professional engineer or for certification as an engineering intern
31 shall apply for registration or certification on a form prescribed
32 and provided by the board. Each application for registration shall
33 contain statements showing the education and qualifications of the
34 applicant and a detailed summary of the technical work performed
35 by the applicant. An application for registration as a professional
36 engineer shall be accompanied by the names of five (5) persons to
37 be used as references, at least three (3) of whom are professional
38 engineers who have a personal knowledge of the experience of the
39 applicant. Each application for a certification as an engineering
40 intern must contain statements showing the education of the
41 applicant, and except for an application for an examination to be
42 given during the applicant's senior year, the application shall be
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

360
1 accompanied by the names of three (3) persons to be used as
2 references. All applications shall be certified to by the applicant as
3 to the correctness of the statements contained in the application.
4 (b) A person who knowingly makes a false statement in the
5 application commits a Class A misdemeanor.
6 (c) The amount of registration fees for a professional engineer
7 and for an engineering intern shall be fixed and determined by the
8 board under IC 25-1-8-2. The board shall also in its rules prescribe
9 the manner and the time for the paying of registration fees.
10 (d) In the event the board refuses to issue a certificate of
11 registration to any person who has made proper application for
12 registration as a professional engineer, any initial fee prescribed by
13 the board and deposited with the board by an applicant shall be
14 retained by the board as an application fee.
15 (e) In the event the board refuses to issue a certificate to any
16 person who has made proper application for certification as an
17 engineering intern, the fee prescribed by the board and deposited
18 by the applicant with the board shall be retained as an application
19 fee.
20 Sec. 5. (a) The examination required of all applicants for
21 registration as a professional engineer shall be a written or
22 computer based examination which shall be divided into the
23 following two (2) parts:
24 (1) Engineering fundamentals.
25 (2) Principles and practice of engineering.
26 The board may adopt rules under IC 4-22-2 establishing additional
27 examination requirements.
28 (b) The engineering fundamentals portion of the examination
29 shall be designed to test the applicant's knowledge of mathematics
30 and the physical and engineering sciences. The standards of
31 proficiency required shall approximate that attained by graduation
32 in an approved four (4) year engineering curriculum.
33 (c) The principles and practice of the engineering portion of the
34 examination shall be designed primarily to test the applicant's
35 understanding of, and judgment and ability to apply correctly, the
36 principles of:
37 (1) mathematics;
38 (2) the physical sciences;
39 (3) the engineering sciences; and
40 (4) engineering design analysis and synthesis;
41 to the practice of professional engineering. A part of the
42 examination may be designed to test the applicant's knowledge and
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

361
1 understanding of the ethical, economic, and legal principles
2 relating to the practices of professional engineering.
3 (d) An applicant for registration as a professional engineer who
4 holds an engineering intern certificate issued in Indiana or in any
5 other state or territory having equivalent standards may be
6 exempted from the engineering fundamentals portion of the
7 examination.
8 (e) An applicant must successfully pass the engineering
9 fundamentals portion of the examination before taking the
10 principles and practice portion of the examination.
11 (f) Examinations shall be held at times and places as determined
12 by the board at least two (2) times each year. Examinations for
13 certification as an engineering intern may be held separately from
14 the examinations for registration as a professional engineer.
15 (g) An applicant for registration as a professional engineer who
16 is presently registered in another state or territory may be assigned
17 a written or computer based examination as the board deems
18 necessary to meet the requirements of this chapter.
19 (h) An applicant shall have three (3) attempts to pass each part
20 of the examination within a time period established by the board
21 by rule. The board may specify by rule the time required between
22 examinations and the opportunity for future examination attempts
23 for an applicant who fails to appear at an examination. The
24 amount of the fee to be paid for each examination shall be
25 determined by the board under IC 25-1-8-2.
26 (i) If an applicant who has failed a part of the examination three
27 (3) times reapplies and submits evidence of acquiring additional
28 knowledge for the examination, the board may give the applicant
29 approval to take subsequent examinations or partial examinations.
30 Sec. 6. (a) The board shall issue a certificate of registration,
31 upon the payment of the fee prescribed by the board under
32 IC 25-1-8-2 and the fee established by section 2(b) of this chapter,
33 to any applicant who, in the opinion of the board, has satisfactorily
34 met all requirements of this chapter. In the case of a professional
35 engineer, the certificate shall authorize the practice of
36 "professional engineering". The certificate of registration shall:
37 (1) show the full name of the registrant;
38 (2) bear a serial number and date; and
39 (3) be signed by a designee of the board.
40 The issuance of any certificate of registration by the board under
41 this chapter is evidence that the individual named on the certificate
42 is entitled to all the rights and privileges of a registered
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

362
1 professional engineer from the date on the certificate until it
2 expires or is revoked.
3 (b) The board shall issue a certificate of enrollment upon the
4 payment of the certificate fee prescribed by the board under
5 IC 25-1-8-2 to any applicant who, in the opinion of the board, has
6 satisfactorily met all of the requirements of this chapter. In the
7 case of an engineering intern, the certificate shall state that the
8 applicant has successfully passed the examination in engineering
9 fundamentals and has been enrolled as an engineering intern. The
10 certificate of enrollment shall:
11 (1) show the full name of the enrollee;
12 (2) bear a serial number and date; and
13 (3) be signed by the director of the licensing agency.
14 The issuance of a certificate of enrollment by the board is evidence
15 that the individual named on the certificate is entitled to all the
16 rights and privileges of an engineering intern while the certificate
17 remains unrevoked or until it expires.
18 Sec. 7. (a) The granting of registration extends to the registrant
19 the authority to use a seal of a design approved by the board
20 bearing the registrant's name, registration number, and the legend
21 "professional engineer".
22 (b) During the period of time that a registrant's certificate is
23 valid, the registrant is authorized to apply the registrant's seal to
24 plans, specifications, studies, drawings, and reports. Applying the
25 registrant's seal attests that:
26 (1) the work embodies the engineering work of the registrant;
27 (2) the registrant or an employed subordinate supervised by
28 the registrant prepared the documents, and in the context of
29 engineered plans "prepared" refers to the registrant's control
30 and direction of the engineering work and design process;
31 (3) the registrant assumes full professional responsibility for
32 the documents; and
33 (4) the work meets standards of acceptable engineering
34 practice.
35 (c) It is unlawful for any person to stamp or seal any document
36 with a seal after the certificate of the registrant named on the seal
37 has expired or has been revoked.
38 Sec. 8. (a) Subject to IC 25-1-2-6(e), unless renewed, a certificate
39 issued under this chapter expires on a date specified by the
40 licensing agency under IC 25-1-6-4 and expires biennially after the
41 initial expiration date. An applicant for renewal shall submit an
42 application in the manner prescribed by the board and pay the
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

363
1 renewal fee established by the board under IC 25-1-8-2 on or
2 before the renewal date specified by the licensing agency.
3 (b) Subject to IC 25-1-2-6(e), if the holder of a certificate does
4 not renew the certificate by the date specified by the licensing
5 agency, the certificate expires and becomes invalid without the
6 board taking any action.
7 (c) The failure on the part of a registrant to renew a certificate
8 does not deprive the registrant of the right of renewal.
9 (d) If a certificate has been expired for not more than three (3)
10 years, the certificate may be reinstated by the board if the holder
11 of the certificate meets the requirements for reinstatement under
12 IC 25-1-8-6(c).
13 (e) If a certificate has been expired for more than three (3)
14 years, the certificate may be reinstated by the board if the holder
15 of the certificate meets the requirements for reinstatement under
16 IC 25-1-8-6(d).
17 Sec. 9. (a) The board may adopt rules requiring a professional
18 engineer to obtain continuing education for renewal of a certificate
19 under section 8 of this chapter.
20 (b) If the board adopts rules under this section, the rules must
21 do the following:
22 (1) Establish an inactive certificate of registration that:
23 (A) does not require the holder of an inactive certificate to
24 obtain continuing education; and
25 (B) prohibits the holder of an inactive certificate from
26 practicing engineering.
27 (2) Establish requirements for reactivation of an inactive
28 certificate.
29 Sec. 10. (a) A registration certificate for a professional engineer
30 may be issued only to a natural person.
31 (b) A business, including a proprietorship, partnership, or
32 corporation, doing business in Indiana may not practice or offer to
33 practice engineering unless that practice is carried on under the
34 responsible direction and supervision of a registered professional
35 engineer who is a full-time employee of the business. All plans,
36 sheets of designs, specifications, reports, studies, or other
37 engineering documents that require certification and are prepared
38 by the personnel of a business must carry the signature and seal of
39 the registered professional engineer who is in responsible charge
40 of the professional engineering work.
41 Sec. 11. (a) A county, city, town, township, school corporation,
42 or other political subdivision of this state may not engage in the
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

364
1 construction or maintenance of any public work involving the
2 practice of engineering for which plans, specifications, and
3 estimates have not been prepared, certified, and sealed by, and the
4 construction and maintenance executed under the direct
5 supervision of, a professional engineer. Any contract executed in
6 violation of this section is void.
7 (b) An official of this state, or of any city, town, county,
8 township, or school corporation, charged with the enforcement of
9 any law, ordinance, or rule relating to the design, construction, or
10 alteration of buildings or structures may not use or accept or
11 approve any plans or specifications that have not been prepared
12 by, or under the supervision of and certified by, a registered
13 professional engineer. This subsection does not apply:
14 (1) to plans or specifications prepared by, or under the
15 supervision of and certified by, an architect who is registered
16 under IC 25-4-1;
17 (2) to structures and construction listed in IC 22-15-3-3(a); or
18 (3) to plans or specifications contained in a registration,
19 license, or permit application, including an application for an
20 initial permit, the renewal of a permit, the modification of a
21 permit, or a variance from a permit submitted to the
22 commissioner of the department of environmental
23 management under IC 13, unless the permit is for the
24 approval of plans or specifications for construction for which
25 a professional engineer's seal is required by operation of
26 either state or federal law, rule, or regulation. This subsection
27 does not require a professional engineer's seal for an
28 application for an air quality construction permit under 326
29 IAC 2-1-3.
30 This section shall not be construed as to abridge or otherwise affect
31 the powers of any state board or department to issue rules
32 governing the safety of buildings or structures.
33 (c) All maps required to show the underground workings of any
34 mine in Indiana must be prepared, certified, and sealed by a
35 professional engineer or professional surveyor.
36 Sec. 12. (a) An employee or a subordinate of any person who
37 holds a certificate of registration under the provisions of this
38 chapter is exempt from the provisions of this chapter if the practice
39 of the employee or subordinate does not include responsible charge
40 of design or supervision.
41 (b) This chapter does not require registration for the purpose of
42 practicing engineering by an individual or a business:
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

365
1 (1) on property owned or leased by that individual or business
2 unless the engineering practice involves the public health or
3 safety, or the health or safety of the employees of that
4 individual or business;
5 (2) for the performance of engineering which relates solely to
6 the design or fabrication of manufactured products; or
7 (3) that is registered as a landscape architect under IC 25-4-2
8 and while the individual or business is engaged in the practice
9 of landscape architecture planning the use of land or water.
10 Sec. 13. The board may, upon application and payment of a fee
11 established by the board in the board's rules, issue a certificate of
12 registration as a professional engineer to an individual who holds
13 a valid certificate of registration as a professional engineer, issued
14 to the applicant by the proper authority of any state or territory or
15 possession of the United States if the requirements for registration
16 of professional engineers that the certificate of registration was
17 issued under do not conflict with the provisions of this chapter. In
18 determining the qualifications of an applicant, the board may
19 accept the verified professional record of the applicant that is
20 certified by the National Council of Examiners for Engineers and
21 Surveyors. However, an applicant meets the experience
22 requirement under section 3 of this chapter if the applicant:
23 (1) has at least three (3) years of engineering work experience
24 after the applicant graduates from an approved engineering
25 curriculum but before the applicant successfully passes an
26 examination required under section 5 of this chapter; and
27 (2) has been registered or licensed as a professional engineer
28 in another state for at least ten (10) years.
29 Sec. 14. A certificate of registration, or a certification as an
30 engineering intern to replace a certificate which has been lost,
31 destroyed, or mutilated, may be issued subject to the rules of the
32 board and the fee established under IC 25-1-8-2.
33 Sec. 15. A person who:
34 (1) practices or offers to practice engineering without being
35 registered or exempted under the laws of this state;
36 (2) presents as the person's own the certificate of registration
37 or the seal of another;
38 (3) gives any false or forged evidence of any kind to the board
39 or to any member of the board in obtaining a certificate of
40 registration;
41 (4) impersonates any other registrant;
42 (5) uses an expired, suspended, or revoked certificate of
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

366
1 registration; or
2 (6) otherwise violates this chapter;
3 commits a Class B misdemeanor.
4 Sec. 16. (a) It is the duty of all law enforcement officers of this
5 state, or any political subdivision, to enforce the provisions of this
6 chapter and to apprehend and prosecute any person who violates
7 any of the provisions of this chapter.
8 (b) The attorney general shall act as the legal advisor of the
9 board and render any legal assistance as may be necessary in
10 carrying out the provisions of this chapter.
11 (c) The attorney general and the licensing agency may use the
12 registered professional engineers and registered engineering
13 interns investigative fund established by section 20 of this chapter
14 to hire investigators and other employees to enforce the provisions
15 of this chapter and to investigate and prosecute violations of this
16 chapter.
17 Sec. 17. (a) The attorney general, the prosecuting attorney of
18 any county, the board, or a citizen of any county where a person
19 who is not exempted engages in the practice of engineering without
20 first having obtained a certificate of registration or without first
21 having renewed an expired certificate of registration may, in
22 accordance with the provisions of the laws of this state governing
23 injunctions, file an action in the name of the state of Indiana to
24 enjoin that person from engaging in the practice of engineering
25 until a certificate of registration is secured or renewed.
26 (b) Any person who has been enjoined and violates an injunction
27 shall be punished for contempt of court. An injunction does not
28 relieve a person engaged in the practice of engineering without a
29 certificate of registration or without first having renewed an
30 expired certificate of registration from a criminal prosecution.
31 (c) The remedy by injunction is in addition to any remedy
32 provided for herein for the criminal prosecution of the offender. In
33 charging any person in a complaint for violation of the provisions
34 of this chapter by engaging in the practice of engineering without
35 a certificate of registration or without having renewed an expired
36 certificate of registration, it is sufficient to charge that the
37 offender:
38 (1) on a certain day in a certain county practiced or offered to
39 practice engineering; and
40 (2) was not registered or exempted under this chapter.
41 Sec. 18. (a) Except as provided in IC 25-4-1-11, a person
42 registered as a professional engineer under this chapter is exempt
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

367
1 from the provisions of any and all statutes in force in this state
2 regulating the practice of architecture.
3 (b) This chapter does not apply to an individual registered as an
4 architect under IC 25-4-1. An architect, however, who is registered
5 under IC 25-4-1 and exempted from this chapter may not use the
6 designation "engineer" in any form or manner unless the architect
7 is registered under this chapter.
8 Sec. 19. (a) This section does not apply to a job position filled by
9 the department of environmental management.
10 (b) Unless a job position is filled by a professional engineer, the
11 state or a political subdivision (as defined in IC 36-1-2-13) may not
12 use the word "engineer" in the position's job title.
13 Sec. 20. (a) The registered professional engineers and registered
14 engineering interns investigative fund is established to provide
15 funds for administering and enforcing the provisions of this
16 chapter, including investigating and taking enforcement action
17 against violators of this chapter. The fund shall be administered by
18 the attorney general and the licensing agency.
19 (b) The expenses of administering the fund shall be paid from
20 the money in the fund. The fund consists of money from the fee
21 imposed upon registered professional engineers and registered
22 engineering interns under section 9(b) of this chapter.
23 (c) The treasurer of state shall invest the money in the fund not
24 currently needed to meet the obligations of the fund in the same
25 manner as other public money may be invested.
26 (d) Money in the fund at the end of a state fiscal year does not
27 revert to the state general fund. If the total amount in the fund
28 exceeds five hundred thousand dollars ($500,000) at the end of a
29 state fiscal year after payment of all claims and expenses, the
30 amount that exceeds five hundred thousand dollars ($500,000)
31 reverts to the state general fund.
32 (e) Money in the fund is continually appropriated for use by the
33 attorney general and the licensing agency to administer and
34 enforce the provisions of this chapter and to conduct investigations
35 and take enforcement action against persons violating the
36 provisions of this chapter.
37 Sec. 21. The board shall send written notice to the department
38 of homeland security established by IC 10-19-2-1 of the
39 determination of the board in a disciplinary action against an
40 engineer under IC 25-1-11. The board shall send the notice not
41 later than three (3) business days after the date of the board's
42 determination.

27 (B) hold the degree of doctor of medicine or its equivalent;
28 (C) hold valid unlimited licenses to practice medicine in
29 Indiana;
30 (D) have been practicing physicians for at least five (5)
31 years; and
32 (E) are residents of Indiana.
33 (2) One (1) reputable osteopathic physician who:
34 (A) is a graduate of an accredited osteopathic medical
35 school;
36 (B) holds the degree of doctor of osteopathy or its
37 equivalent;
38 (C) holds a valid unlimited license to practice osteopathic
39 medicine in Indiana;
40 (D) have been practicing osteopathic physicians for at least
41 five (5) years; and
42 (E) are residents of Indiana.

21 audiology in any way other than as a consumer. Except for the member
22 representing the general public, All board members shall at all times be

34 for speech-language and hearing may submit to the governor its

388
1 to prepare, approve, grade, or administer examinations or, upon request

6 members. A majority of the quorum may transact business. The
7 chairperson of the board is not allowed to cast a vote on any matter

16 SECTION 11, IS AMENDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
17 JANUARY 1, 2027]: Sec. 8. (a) As used in this section, "emergency
18 medical services" has the meaning set forth in IC 16-18-2-110.
19 (b) As used in this section, "emergency medical services provider
20 organization" means a provider of emergency medical services that is
21 certified by the Indiana emergency medical services fire and EMS
22 standards and education commission as an advanced life support
23 provider organization under rules adopted under IC 16-31-3.
24 (c) As used in this section, "policy of accident and sickness
25 insurance" has the meaning set forth in IC 27-8-5-1. However, for
26 purposes of this section, the term does not include the following:
27 (1) Accident only, credit, dental, vision, Medicare supplement,
28 long term care, or disability income insurance.
29 (2) Coverage issued as a supplement to liability insurance.
30 (3) Automobile medical payment insurance.
31 (4) A specified disease policy.
32 (5) A policy that provides a stipulated daily, weekly, or monthly
33 payment to an insured without regard to the actual expense of the
34 confinement.
35 (6) A short term insurance plan (as defined in IC 27-8-5.9-3).
36 (d) A policy of accident and sickness insurance that provides
37 coverage for emergency medical services must provide reimbursement
38 for emergency medical services that are:
39 (1) rendered by an emergency medical services provider
40 organization;
41 (2) within the emergency medical services provider organization's
42 scope of practice;
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

395
1 (3) performed or provided as advanced life support services; and
2 (4) performed or provided:
3 (A) during a response initiated through the 911 system; or
4 (B) as part of a mobile integrated healthcare program described
5 in IC 16-31-12;
6 regardless of whether the patient is transported.
7 (e) Reimbursement for basic and advanced life support services
8 through a policy to which this section applies must be provided on an
9 equal basis regardless of whether the services involve transportation of
10 the patient by ambulance.
11 (f) If multiple emergency medical services provider organizations
12 qualify and submit a claim for reimbursement under this section for an
13 encounter, the insurer:
14 (1) may reimburse under this section only for one (1) claim per
15 patient encounter; and
16 (2) shall reimburse the claim submitted by the emergency medical
17 services provider organization that performed or provided the
18 majority of advanced life support services for the patient.
19 (g) The department may adopt rules under IC 4-22-2 to implement
20 this section.
21 (h) This section does not require a policy of accident and sickness
22 insurance to provide coverage for emergency medical services.

42 keep the technology used by county recorders in Indiana compatible
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

409
1 with technology used by recording offices in other jurisdictions that

25 (2) an appeal from a final determination made by the worker's
26 compensation board;
27 (3) an appeal of a final determination made by the department of
28 state revenue; or
29 (4) an appeal of a final determination made by the department of
30 workforce development or the department of workforce
31 development unemployment insurance review board.
32 (b) However, this chapter does not apply to an order or other
33 determination:
34 (1) under:

36 (B) IC 16-28;
37 (C) IC 16-30;
38 (D) IC 12-28-4; or
39 (E) IC 12-28-5;

13 SECTION 742. IC 36-8-2.5-1, AS AMENDED BY P.L.7-2021,
14 SECTION 2, IS AMENDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
15 JANUARY 1, 2027]: Sec. 1. The following definitions apply
16 throughout this chapter:
17 (1) "Communication" means an oral or written statement, note,
18 record, recording, report, or document made during, or arising out
19 of, a first responder's receipt of CISM services or peer support
20 services.
21 (2) "Critical incident" means an actual or perceived event or
22 situation that involves crisis, disaster, trauma, or emergency.
23 (3) "Critical incident stress" means the acute or cumulative
24 psychological stress or trauma that a first responder may
25 experience in providing emergency services in response to a
26 critical incident. The stress or trauma is an unusually strong
27 emotional, cognitive, behavioral, or physical reaction that may
28 interfere with normal functioning by causing:
29 (A) physical and emotional injury or illness;
30 (B) failure of a usual coping mechanism;
31 (C) loss of interest in work or normal life activities;
32 (D) loss of ability to function; or
33 (E) psychological disruption of personal life, including the
34 emergency responder's relationship with a spouse, child,
35 relative, or friend.
36 (4) "CISM services" means critical incident stress management
37 services that include programs and services related to education,
38 prevention, and mitigation of the effects from exposure to highly
39 stressful critical incidents. The term includes services provided by
40 an individual certified to provide group crisis intervention,
41 individual crisis intervention, peer support, or any related service.
42 (5) "CISM services provider" means an individual who is certified
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

430
1 to provide CISM services and who is acting as part of a CISM
2 team or in another official capacity.
3 (6) "CISM team" means an organized community or local crisis
4 response team that is trained and certified under standards
5 substantially similar to the training and certification standards
6 established by the Indiana emergency medical services fire and
7 EMS standards and education commission as set forth under
8 IC 16-31-2.
9 (7) "First responder" has the meaning set forth in IC 10-10.5-2-1.
10 (8) "First responder recipient" means an individual who is a first
11 responder and receives CISM services, peer support services, or
12 both.
13 (9) "Peer support services" means services provided by a person
14 who:
15 (A) is a first responder working for the same department or
16 public agency that the first responder recipient works for; and
17 (B) acts:
18 (i) in the person's official capacity as a peer support team
19 member; and
20 (ii) within the written peer support guidelines that are in
21 effect for the person's respective first responder agency
22 program.
23 The term includes active listening, assessment, mentoring, crisis
24 intervention, or other support services provided to an employee
25 experiencing personal or professional difficulties that may or may
26 not be a result of the employee's exposure to highly stressful
27 critical incidents.
28 (10) "Peer support team member" means a first responder who:
29 (A) has successfully completed peer support training; and
30 (B) is designated a peer support team member;
31 by the head of the first responder's agency or the president of the
32 bargaining unit of the employees of the department or agency.
33 SECTION 743. IC 36-8-3-4.3, AS AMENDED BY P.L.57-2023,
34 SECTION 3, IS AMENDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
35 JANUARY 1, 2027]: Sec. 4.3. (a) This section also applies to a town,
36 township, or fire department of a fire protection territory or fire
37 protection district, that has at least one (1) certified employee of a

39 (1) the employee is an appointed police officer or firefighter; or
40 (2) under section 5 of this chapter, the police or fire department
41 is exempt from sections 3, 4, and 4.1 of this chapter.

HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

431
1 who, as a condition of employment, holds a valid certification issued
2 under IC 16-31-3 by the Indiana emergency medical services fire and
3 EMS standards and education commission established by
4 IC 16-31-2-1. IC 16-31-2-1.5.
5 (c) As used in this section, "medical director" means a physician
6 with an unlimited license to practice medicine in Indiana and who
7 performs the duties and responsibilities described in 836 IAC 2-2-1.
8 (d) If a medical director takes any of the following actions against
9 a certified employee, the medical director shall provide to the certified
10 employee and to the chief of the certified employee's department a
11 written explanation of the reasons for the action taken by the medical
12 director:
13 (1) The medical director refuses or fails to supervise or otherwise
14 provide medical control and direction to the certified employee.
15 (2) The medical director refuses or fails to attest to the
16 competency of the certified employee to perform emergency
17 medical services.
18 (3) The medical director suspends the certified employee from
19 performing emergency medical services.
20 (e) Before a police or fire department takes any employment related
21 action against a certified employee as the result of a medical director's
22 action described in subsection (d), the certified employee is entitled to
23 a hearing and appeal concerning the medical director's action as
24 provided in section 4 of this chapter.
25 (f) If the medical director's action that is the subject of an appeal
26 under subsection (e) is based on a health care decision made by the
27 certified employee in performing emergency medical services, the
28 safety board conducting the hearing shall consult with an independent
29 medical expert to determine whether the certified employee followed
30 the applicable emergency medical services protocol in making the
31 health care decision. The independent medical expert:
32 (1) must be a physician trained in emergency medical services;
33 and
34 (2) may not be affiliated with the same hospital as the medical
35 director.

15 (1) a political subdivision;
16 (2) an individual or the legal representative of a deceased
17 individual;
18 (3) a firm;
19 (4) an association;
20 (5) a limited liability company;
21 (6) an employer that provides on-the-job training under the
22 federal School to Work Opportunities Act (20 U.S.C. 6101 et
23 seq.) to the extent set forth in IC 22-3-2-2.5(a); or
24 (7) a corporation or its receiver or trustee;
25 that uses the services of another person for pay.
26 "Essential employee" means an employee:
27 (1) who the employer has determined to be essential to the
28 operation of the employer's daily enterprise; and
29 (2) without whom the employer is likely to suffer economic injury
30 as a result of the absence of the essential employee.
31 "Nominal compensation" means annual compensation of not more
32 than twenty thousand dollars ($20,000).
33 "Public servant" has the meaning set forth in IC 35-31.5-2-261.
34 "Responsible party" has the meaning set forth in IC 13-11-2-191(d).
35 "Volunteer fire department" means a department or association
36 organized for the purpose of answering fire alarms, extinguishing fires,
37 and providing other emergency services, the majority of members of
38 which receive no compensation or nominal compensation for their
39 services.
40 "Volunteer firefighter" means a firefighter:
41 (1) who, as a result of a written application, has been elected or
42 appointed to membership in a volunteer fire department;
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

437
1 (2) who has executed a pledge to faithfully perform, with or
2 without nominal compensation, the work related duties assigned
3 and orders given to the firefighter by the chief of the volunteer
4 fire department or an officer of the volunteer fire department,
5 including orders or duties involving education and training as
6 prescribed by the volunteer fire department or the state; and
7 (3) whose name has been entered on a roster of volunteer
8 firefighters that is kept by the volunteer fire department and that
9 has been approved by the proper officers of the unit.
10 "Volunteer member" means a member of a volunteer emergency
11 medical services association connected with a unit as set forth in
12 IC 16-31-5-1(6).

6 SECTION 760. IC 36-9-27-71 IS AMENDED TO READ AS
7 FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE JANUARY 1, 2027]: Sec. 71. (a) When, in
8 the construction or reconstruction of a regulated drain, the county
9 surveyor determines that the proposed drain will cross a public
10 highway or the right-of-way of a railroad company at a point where:
11 (1) there is no crossing; or
12 (2) the crossing will not adequately handle or will be endangered
13 by the flow of water from the drain when completed;
14 the county surveyor shall include in the plans the grade and cross
15 section requirements for a new crossing, or the requirements for
16 altering, enlarging, repairing, or replacing the crossing. The surveyor
17 shall mail a copy of the requirements addressed to the owner of the
18 highway or right-of-way.
19 (b) When requested by the owner of the highway or right-of-way,
20 the county surveyor shall meet with the owner at a time and place to be
21 fixed by the surveyor. The surveyor shall hear objections to the
22 requirements, and may then change the requirements as justice may
23 require.
24 (c) When the board finds that in the construction, reconstruction, or
25 maintenance of a regulated drain it is necessary to:
26 (1) alter, enlarge, repair, or replace a crossing; or
27 (2) construct a new crossing where none existed before;
28 the cost of the work on the crossing shall be paid by the owner of the
29 public highway. This cost may not be considered by the county
30 surveyor or by the board in determining the cost of the work on the
31 drain or in assessing benefits and damages. However, if it is necessary
32 for the owner of a public highway to construct a new crossing because
33 of a cut-off for the purpose of shortening or straightening a regulated
34 drain, the owner of the public highway shall pay one-half (1/2) of the
35 cost of the new crossing, and the remainder shall be included in the
36 cost of the work on the drain.
37 (d) A railroad company with a right-of-way that is:
38 (1) crossed by the construction of a regulated drain; or
39 (2) affected by the altering or enlarging of a crossing;
40 shall pay one-half (1/2) of the cost of the work on the crossing and the
41 remainder shall be included in the cost of the work on the drain.
42 (e) If the county surveyor is registered under IC 25-31, IC 25-4-3,
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

444
1 the county surveyor must review and approve or disapprove the plans

5 county surveyor shall disapprove the plans and hydraulic data if they
6 do not show that the structure will meet hydraulic requirements that

COMMITTEE REPORT
Mr. Speaker: Your Committee on Ways and Means, to which was
referred House Bill 1003, has had the same under consideration and
begs leave to report the same back to the House with the
recommendation that said bill be amended as follows:
Page 7, between lines 33 and 34, begin a new paragraph and insert:
"SECTION 3. IC 2-5-53.5 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA CODE
AS A NEW CHAPTER TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
UPON PASSAGE]:
Chapter 53.5. Government Reform Task Force
Sec. 1. As used in this chapter, "agency" has the meaning set
forth in IC 4-22-2-3.
Sec. 2. As used in this chapter, "council" refers to the legislative
council established by IC 2-5-1.1-1.
Sec. 3. As used in this chapter, "rule" has the meaning set forth
in IC 4-22-2-3.
Sec. 4. As used in this chapter, "task force" refers to the
government reform task force established by section 5 of this
chapter.
Sec. 5. The government reform task force is established.
Sec. 6. (a) Except as provided in subsections (b), (c), (d), and (e),
IC 2-5-1.2 applies to the task force.
(b) The task force consists of the following ten (10) members of
the general assembly:
(1) Three (3) members appointed by the president pro
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

458
tempore of the senate.
(2) Two (2) members appointed by the minority leader of the
senate.
(3) Three (3) members appointed by the speaker of the house
of representatives.
(4) Two (2) members appointed by the minority leader of the
house of representatives.
A member of the task force serves at the pleasure of the appointing
authority.
(c) The task force shall meet as called by the chair. All meetings
of the task force shall be open to the public in accordance with and
subject to IC 5-14-1.5. All records of the task force shall be subject
to the requirements of IC 5-14-3.
(d) A majority of the members of the task force constitutes a
quorum. The affirmative vote of at least a majority of the members
at a meeting at which a quorum is present is necessary for the task
force to take official action other than to meet and take testimony.
(e) The:
(1) chairman of the council shall designate the chair; and
(2) vice chairman of the council shall designate the vice chair;
of the task force from the members of the task force. The chair and
vice chair of the task force serve as chair and vice chair at the
pleasure of the appointing authority.
Sec. 7. The task force shall study:
(1) the feasibility of transferring the administration of the
office of the state chemist from Purdue University to the
Indiana state department of agriculture;
(2) any employment issue arising from a transfer of the
administration of the office of the state chemist; and
(3) any issue arising from a transfer of any property, records,
funds, or accounts of the office of state chemist to the Indiana
state department of agriculture.
Sec. 8. The task force may request information or testimony
from department or agency heads, or the designee of a department
or agency head, as determined necessary by the task force.
Sec. 9. The task force shall:
(1) develop recommendations in a report concerning the
issues set forth in section 7 of this chapter; and
(2) submit the report to the council in an electronic format
under IC 5-14-6 not later than November 1, 2026.
Sec. 10. The legislative services agency shall provide support
staff to the task force.
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

459
Sec. 11. This chapter expires June 30, 2027.".
Page 10, delete lines 29 through 42.
Page 11, delete lines 1 through 18.
Page 64, line 8, after "the" insert "Indiana".
Page 110, between lines 2 and 3, begin a new paragraph and insert:
"SECTION 195. IC 14-21-1-22.5 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA
CODE AS A NEW SECTION TO READ AS FOLLOWS
[EFFECTIVE JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 22.5. (a) The review board shall
establish the Indiana historical marker program for marking
historical sites in Indiana. As a part of this program, the review
board shall fix a state format for historical markers. No person
may erect a historical marker in the state format without the
approval of the review board. All historical markers in the state
format shall be provided by the review board using appropriated
funds, local matching funds, donations, grants, or any other funds
provided for that purpose according to the guidelines and rules of
the historical marker program.
(b) The review board may appoint a historical marker advisory
committee to serve without compensation. The committee may
advise the review board and the director concerning the following:
(1) Guidelines and rules for the historical marker program.
(2) Appropriate sites to be marked.
(3) Other matters concerning the historical marker program
as requested by the review board or the director.
(c) Historical markers approved under this section, including
state format markers installed after 1945 and markers installed by
the Indiana Civil War centennial commission, are the property of
the state. Maintenance of state historical markers is part of the
historical marker program. The review board may cooperate with
individuals, local and state agencies, and private institutions and
organizations for the maintenance of the historical markers. Funds
made available to the historical marker program, as approved by
the review board, may be used for necessary maintenance.
(d) No historical marker may be erected on a highway of the
state highway system without the approval of the review board as
to its historical accuracy. This provision is in addition to any other
requirement of law.".
